evidence: The paper uses "mean decrease in Gini impurity" to rank features, stating "Pre-pregnancy BMI, BMI at sample collection visit, and pre-pregnancy weight ranked among the top 5 most important features" and identifies Peptostreptococcus and Mycoplasma as "highest importance scores." However, authors corroborate top-ranked taxa through ANCOM-BC2 differential abundance (FDR q=0.049 for Mycoplasma; within-fold p<0.10 for Peptostreptococcus across all 5 folds) and explicitly acknowledge "cross-validation stability within a single 43-subject cohort does not substitute for external validation," framing findings as hypothesis-generating.
